---
id: interact-with-post
title: "Post-type-specific interaction"

import MethodEndpoint from "@theme/ApiExplorer/MethodEndpoint";
import ParamsDetails from "@theme/ParamsDetails";
import RequestSchema from "@theme/RequestSchema";
import StatusCodes from "@theme/StatusCodes";
import OperationTabs from "@theme/OperationTabs";
import TabItem from "@theme/TabItem";
import Heading from "@theme/Heading";
import Translate from "@docusaurus/Translate";

<Heading
  as={"h1"}
  className={"openapi__heading"}
  children={"Post-type-specific interaction"}
>
</Heading>

<MethodEndpoint
  method={"post"}
  path={"/api/posts/{postId}/interactions"}
  context={"endpoint"}
>
  
</MethodEndpoint>



A generic, per-user action whose meaning depends on the post's
`type` — poll voting and trip check-ins today, more later as new
types are registered. `key`/`value` are opaque here; the post's own
type decides what's valid. This endpoint itself emits no
notification — a handler that wants one sends it itself (a trip
check-in does, see below).

For a `POLL` post: `key: "vote"`, `value: { optionId }`. Voting your
current option again **removes** your vote (unvote); voting a
different option **switches** it. Votes are public to group members
(not anonymous) and results are visible whether or not you've voted.
Voting after `closesAt` has passed is rejected.

For a `TRIP` post, three keys are supported:

- `key: "checkin"`, `value: { place, text?, photoUrls? }` — adds a
  journal entry (`place` 1-80 chars, `text` up to 2000 chars,
  `photoUrls` up to 10 uploaded `/uploads/...` paths). Only the
  trip's author or one of its `travelerUserIds` may check in, and
  only while the trip is open. Notifies the rest of the group with a
  push-only `trip_checkin` notification (same-day check-ins from the
  same author are bundled into one notification).
- `key: "close"`, no `value` — closes the trip early. Author only,
  and only once (a trip also auto-closes once its `endDate` has
  fully elapsed).
- `key: "setTravelers"`, `value: { userIds }` — replaces the trip's
  co-traveler list wholesale (up to 20 ids; the author is always an
  implicit traveler). Author only, and only while the trip is open;
  every id must be a current member of the trip's group.

  id={"request"}
  as={"h2"}
